results.possibleIdentity: Ansel H. Leffel (1927–2016) was a mechanical engineer based in New Jersey during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by the Lockheed Electronics Company, specifically within its Military Systems division located in Plainfield, New Jersey (a location often associated with neighboring Dunellen). In December 1967, Leffel was part of a technical team at Lockheed that produced a significant NASA-monitored report (NASA-CR-86036) titled 'Development of Pictorial Landing Display,' which detailed rapid scan radar and display techniques designed to assist aircraft landings in zero-visibility conditions. Leffel was also part of the technical registry for Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded to foster collaboration between artists and the technical community. His inclusion in the E.A.T. archives, typically maintained via index cards, listed his expertise in mechanical engineering and his professional base in the Dunellen/Plainfield area. He later resided in Ontario, Oregon, where he passed away in 2016.
